arabic_meaning: hare, rabbit (الأرنب: حيوان ذو أذنين طويلتين)
expected_reasoning: Direct animal name preservation. Arabic arnab and Gothic arniba both name the same animal with identical consonant skeleton r-n-b. The word traveled through Semitic trade/cultural contact.
Religious term transmitted through Semitic→Aramaic→Greek→Gothic. The concept of a designated rest day preserved with s-b-t consonants.
Trade good term. Arabic/Persian ibrisim (fine silk) → transmitted through Mediterranean trade routes. Both name the same luxury textile material.

arabic_meaning: to love, hold dear, seed/grain (أحب: أراد ومال إليه، الحبة: البذرة)
expected_reasoning: The Arabic root حب encodes holding/grasping at its core — both loving (holding dear) and grain (what you hold/gather). Gothic haba (to have/hold) preserves the physical holding concept while Arabic kept the emotional extension. ḥ→h merger is documented.

arabic_meaning: to judge, govern, be wise (الحكمة: العلم والفقه، الحاكم: القاضي)
expected_reasoning: Governance/judgment concept preserved with h-k-m consonants through ḥ→h merger. Arabic حكم = to judge/govern, Gothic preserves the judicial concept.

arabic_meaning: sheath, cover, envelope (غلاف: ما يُغطى به الشيء)
expected_reasoning: Arabic غلاف = covering/sheath (what wraps around something). Old English glof = glove (what wraps around the hand). Semantic specialization: general covering → hand covering. gh-l-f → g-l-f through غ→g merger.

arabic_meaning: tower, fortress, elevated structure (البرج: البناء العالي المحصن)
expected_reasoning: Arabic برج = fortified tower/elevated structure. Old English burg = fortified settlement. Both encode the concept of a defensively elevated structure. b-r-j → b-r-g through ج→g merger.
Botanical/trade term. Arabic balsam plant → transmitted through trade routes. Both name the same aromatic resin with identical consonant skeleton.

arabic_meaning: sin, transgression, moral wrong (الإثم: الذنب والمعصية)
expected_reasoning: Moral/religious concept preserved. Arabic إثم = sin/transgression, Old Irish etham = sin. Identical moral-wrong domain with th-m consonant match.

arabic_meaning: to remain, stay, abide; eternity (أبد: أقام به، الأبد: الدهر)
expected_reasoning: Arabic أبد = to remain/stay in a place, eternity. Old English abide = to remain/dwell. Same core concept of permanent remaining with b-d preserved. Found in Gothic (bad), Old English (abide), and Old Irish (baid) — cross-branch convergence.

arabic_meaning: mill, grinding machine (من طحن: سحق الحب)
expected_reasoning: Both mean 'mill' but the consonant skeletons ṭ-ḥ-n and m-l have no correspondence. ط does not map to m, ح has no target, ن has no target. This is independent naming of the same concept — a translation, not a cognate.
Both mean 'book' but k-t-b has no path to b-k. The consonant order is reversed and ت is missing entirely. OE 'book' comes from beech wood (boc = beech tree, used for writing). Independent origins.
Both mean 'water' but Arabic has a single-consonant root م while OE water has w-t-r. No consonant correspondence. Universal concept independently named.
Both mean 'sun' but sh-m-s has no path to s-n. The consonant structures are entirely different. Independent naming of the same celestial body.
Both mean 'moon' but q-m-r has no path to m-n. Different consonant structures.

Arabic سلاح = weapons (instruments of striking). Gothic slahan = to strike/smite. Semantic path: the instrument of striking → the act of striking. s-l-ḥ → s-l-h through ḥ→h merger. The weapon IS the striking.
Arabic دار = house/dwelling. Gothic daur = door/gate. Semantic drift: the dwelling → its defining feature (the entrance). A house IS known by its door. d-r preserved directly.

Arabic ملج = to suckle/nurse (drawing milk). Old Irish melg = to milk (extracting milk). Same fundamental action from different perspectives: the infant suckling vs the farmer milking. m-l-j → m-l-g through ج→g merger.

arabic_meaning: to blow, spit out, exhale (نفث: نفخ ورمى من فمه)
expected_reasoning: Arabic نفث = to blow out/exhale. Old Irish infet = to exhale/breathe out. Same breathing action with n-f-th → n-f-t through ث→t merger.

arabic_meaning: good, excellent, high quality (جيد: ضد الرديء، الحسن)
expected_reasoning: Arabic جيد = good/excellent. Old English god = good. Direct quality/value concept preserved with j-d → g-d through ج→g merger.

arabic_meaning: house, dwelling, enclosed habitable space (البيت: المسكن)
expected_reasoning: Arabic بيت = house/enclosed dwelling. Old Irish biit = dwelling/existence. The concept of dwelling expanded to existence itself — where you dwell IS where you exist. b-t preserved directly.

arabic_meaning: root meaning includes rest, settling (from رست: استقر)
expected_reasoning: Arabic root encodes settling/becoming still. Old English restan = to rest. Same concept of ceasing motion and settling into stillness. r-s-t-n consonants preserved.

arabic_meaning: laurel tree, aromatic plant (الرند: شجر طيب الرائحة)
expected_reasoning: Arabic رند = laurel tree (aromatic). Old English rind = bark/outer tree layer. Semantic drift: the whole aromatic tree → its defining feature (the aromatic bark/rind). r-n-d preserved directly.

target_meaning: a type of wild garlic plant
expected_reasoning: Consonants match but silence and garlic have no semantic path. Random skeleton overlap.
Smallness and shaking have no semantic path. Random consonant overlap.
Recovery from illness and driven snow have no semantic connection. Random skeleton similarity.
Uselessness/mud and promising have no semantic path whatsoever.
Urination and sabbath rest have no semantic connection. The Arabic word is itself a Persian loanword, not native Arabic.
